title: Eriocaulon morulum
taxonomy: Eriocaulon morulum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Eriocaulaceae, commonly known as the pipewort family. This family is part of the order Poales, which includes grasses and other plants with similar characteristics. Eriocaulon morulum is further classified as belonging to the genus Eriocaulon, which comprises around 450 species of plants found primarily in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant's classification reflects its unique growth habits and adaptations to its environment. Within the Eriocaulaceae family, Eriocaulon morulum is one of the many species that have evolved to thrive in a variety of ecosystems.

intro: Eriocaulon morulum, commonly known as the "Fleabane Sedge," is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the Eriocaulaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in the treatment of various inflammatory and bacterial infections, due to its purported health benefits, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Eriocaulon morulum include flavonoids, saponins, and other phenolic compounds, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties.
